title: Faulty Rotated Binary Search with Duplicate Minima

statement.markdown: You are given a sorted array that was initially rotated an unknown number of times. Some numbers may repeat and the minimum element is not distinct. Your task is to find the index of the minimum element using Binary Search.
statement.inputFormat: Input is a space separated list of integers representing the array.
statement.outputFormat: The index of the minimum element in the array.
statement.notes: Array length will be at least 3 and numbers will not be negative.

examples.explanation: Minimum number is 1 and its index in the array is 4.
Minimum number is 1 and its index in the array is 2.
Minimum number is 3 (not present) so return 0.

hints.text: When comparing two elements, consider their values and their positions in the array to handle duplicate minima.

editorial.title: Rotated Binary Search with Multiple Minima
editorial.intuition: To find the minimum element using Binary Search, we need to compare adjacent elements at each step.
editorial.bruteForce: Comparing each element with every other element to find the minimum.
editorial.optimalSolution: Start from the middle, if mid is smaller than its next element, the minimum could be on the right half. If mid is not smaller but mid-1 is, the minimum could be on the left half.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: This approach ensures the search space is reduced by half in each step.
commonMistakes: Missing edge cases, ignoring duplicates, or incorrect comparisons.

starterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int findMin(vector<int>& nums); };
starterCode.java: public class Solution { public int search(int[] nums); }
starterCode.python: class Solution: def findMin(self, nums: List[int]) -> int:

buggyStarterCode.cpp: class Solution { public: int findMin(vector<int>& nums) { int low=0,high=nums.size()-1; while(low<=high){ int mid = low + (high - low)/2; if(nums[mid]==nums[low]) return low; if(nums[mid]>nums[low]) low = mid + 1; else high = mid - 1; } return high; } };
buggyStarterCode.java: public class Solution { public int search(int[] nums) { int low=0,high=nums.length-1; while(low<=high){ int mid = low + (high - low)/2; if(nums[mid]==nums[low]) return low; if(nums[mid]>nums[low]) low = mid + 1; else high = mid - 1; } return high; } }
buggyStarterCode.python: class Solution: def findMin(self, nums: List[int]) -> int: low, high = 0, len(nums)-1; while low <= high: mid = low + (high - low)//2; if nums[mid] == nums[low]: return low; if nums[mid] > nums[low]: low = mid + 1; else: high = mid - 1; return high;
buggyStarterCode.javascript: var findMin = function(nums) { var low=0,high=nums.length-1; while(low<=high){var mid = low + (high - low)/2; if(nums[mid]==nums[low]) return low; if(nums[mid]>nums[low]) low = mid + 1; else high = mid - 1; } return high; }
